title: Large Values, Zero Density, and the Riemann Zeta Function — Proof DAG
description: The classical pipeline of multiplicative number theory: exponent pairs / exponential-sum bounds → growth exponent μ(σ) for ζ(σ+it) → large value theorems LV(σ,τ) for Dirichlet polynomials → zero density estimates A(σ) for ζ → primes in short intervals. Two structurally different engines feed the same targets — Fourier decoupling (Bourgain 2014) and the singular-value/trace method (Guth–Maynard 2024) — and Tao–Trudgian–Yang (2025) systematise the whole web. Cross-links: shares the nodes 1408.5794 and 2405.20552 with the bourgain-demeter-decoupling DAG (the decoupling → ζ → large-values descent); soft addresses_same_question link to maynard-small-gaps (Maynard works in both the bounded-gaps-sieve and the prime-distribution programs, but they are logically disjoint).

1408.5794.title: Decoupling, exponential sums and the Riemann zeta function
1408.5794.role: decoupling-exit — |ζ(1/2+it)| ≪ t^{13/84+ε} via decoupling-derived exponential sums; SHARED node with the bourgain-demeter-decoupling DAG

2405.20552.title: New large value estimates for Dirichlet polynomials
2405.20552.role: headline result — new large-value estimate (trace method) ⟹ zero density A(σ) ≤ 15/(3+5σ) ⟹ primes in x^{17/30+ε} intervals; SHARED node with the bourgain-demeter-decoupling DAG

2501.16779.title: New exponent pairs, zero density estimates, and zero additive energy estimates: a systematic approach
2501.16779.role: synthesis hub — catalogues exponent pairs → μ(σ) → LV(σ,τ) → A(σ) → additive energy, includes the classical bounds and Guth–Maynard, and adds new exponent pairs / improved Heath-Brown & Bourgain zero-density bounds

2503.07410.title: Large value estimates in number theory, harmonic analysis, and computer science
2503.07410.role: survey/map — the large-value problem across number theory, restriction theory/PDE, statistics and complexity theory; classical methods (orthogonality, power, Montgomery–Halász MM*), the Schatten-tensor (trace) method, barriers from SOS and Kakeya

2403.13157.title: A note on zero density results implying large value estimates for Dirichlet polynomials
2403.13157.role: the converse direction — zero density estimates ⟹ large value estimates; a stronger density hypothesis ⟹ primes in intervals of length (log X)^{2+ε}

2411.01845.title: A note on zero-density approaches for the difference between consecutive primes
2411.01845.role: the prime-gaps exit — how zero density estimates (Ingham's classical argument; the weighted Heath-Brown–Iwaniec and Baker–Harman refinements) yield primes in short intervals

related_dags.bourgain-demeter-decoupling: Shares the paper nodes 1408.5794 and 2405.20552. The decoupling DAG carries the upstream story (multilinear Kakeya → ℓ² decoupling → Bourgain's ζ-curve decoupling → exponential-sum bound); this DAG carries the downstream story (large values → zero density → primes). Together they trace decoupling-techniques all the way to prime distribution.
related_dags.maynard-small-gaps: Soft addresses_same_question link only — James Maynard works in both the bounded-gaps Selberg-sieve program (that DAG) and the prime-distribution / zero-density program (this DAG), but the two are logically disjoint mathematics. No theorem-level edge; the connection is authorship/program, not dependency.

Ingham:1940.title: On the estimation of N(σ,T)
Ingham:1940.role: foundational — first nontrivial zero-density estimate A(σ) ≤ 3/(2−σ)

Halasz:1969.title: Über die Mittelwerte multiplikativer zahlentheoretischer Funktionen
Halasz:1969.role: foundational — Halász's large-value/mean-value method (the MM* method)

Montgomery:LargeValues.title: Topics in Multiplicative Number Theory (large value conjecture; Ch. on mean and large values of Dirichlet series)
Montgomery:LargeValues.role: foundational — Montgomery's large value conjecture LV(σ,τ) = min(2−2σ, τ)

Huxley:ExpSums.title: Exponential sums and the Riemann zeta function V
Huxley:ExpSums.role: foundational — Huxley's exponent-pair / ζ-growth bounds and zero-density bound A(σ) ≤ 3/(3σ−1)

HeathBrown:ZeroDensity1979.title: Zero-density estimates for the Riemann zeta-function and Dirichlet L-functions
HeathBrown:ZeroDensity1979.role: foundational — the Heath-Brown zero-density relation and large-value estimate

HeathBrownIwaniec:1979.title: On the difference between consecutive primes
HeathBrownIwaniec:1979.role: foundational — weighted zero-density estimates, primes in x^{0.55+ε} intervals

Bourgain:LargeValues2000.title: On large values estimates for Dirichlet polynomials and the density hypothesis for the Riemann zeta function
Bourgain:LargeValues2000.role: foundational — Bourgain's large-value theorem and density-hypothesis range

Bourgain:Remarks1995.title: Remarks on Halász-Montgomery type inequalities
Bourgain:Remarks1995.role: foundational — the Bourgain zero-density theorem from exponent pairs

ExponentPairTheory.title: Van der Corput's method of exponential sums (A- and B-processes; Phillips, Rankin, Graham–Kolesnik)

BakerHarman:1996.title: The difference between consecutive primes
BakerHarman:1996.role: foundational — Baker–Harman weighted zero-density estimates for short intervals
